The 2026 Swedish Open

A tradition unlike any other.
April 30 · May 1 · May 2 · Trollhättan
Final Standings

The Leaderboard

Final standings after 36 holes. A two-man duel after Espen and Geir withdrew with injuries. Glenn closes nine clear at Koberg with a 89 — his sixth Yellow Jacket. The drinks are on him.

Final Leaderboard
★ Tournament Concluded · 2 May 2026
Pos Player R1 · Onsjö R2 · Koberg Total Status

2 Svein Anders Haugen NORWAY · Runner-up · The Comeback Kid 102 96 198 F
WD Espen Falck Engelstad NORWAY · Withdrew · Back injury — — WD DNS
WD Geir Nærø NORWAY · Withdrew · Leg injury — — WD DNS
Yellow Tees · CR 71.4 / Slope 134 ★ Champion's Yellow Jacket Awarded

"We came to Trollhättan as four. We leave as two on the card — but only one wears the jacket. Det er meg." — Glenn Ulrik Halvorsen, 36th-hole interview

Round One Recap

Onsjö Golfklubb

Thursday, 30 April 2026. Yellow tees. Westerly breeze, firm fairways, and the unmistakable scent of opening-round nerves.

Round Two · Final

Koberg Golfklubb

Friday, 1 May 2026. Slottsbanen. The Yellow Jacket on the line. Glenn delivers a 89 — seven shots better than Onsjö — to seal the championship.

Hole-by-hole · Round 2 gulrik 89 · Haugen 96

The Field

The Patrons of the Game

Four men. Three days. One Yellow Jacket. The gentlemen of the 2026 Swedish Open.

−180
— Player No. I —
Glenn Ulrik Halvorsen
"gulrik" · Tournament Favourite
★ 2026 CHAMPION ★ Six-time Swedish Open champion (2016, '17, '18 shared, '19, '22, '26). The all-time wins leader has done it again. Arrives by Cayenne. Plays Titleist. Plays for keeps.
6
Titles
96
R1
−180
Odds
+220
— Player No. II —
Espen Falck Engelstad
2023 Champion · The Long Ball
Three-time champion (2012 inaugural in Spain, 2018 shared in USA, 2023 at Onsjö). The Founding Father of the Swedish Open. Long-iron specialist whose victories are spoken of in hushed tones at the Champions Dinner. Finished a respectable third in 2026.
3
Titles
3
Finish
+220
Odds
+400
— Player No. III —
Geir Nærø
"The Outsider" · Lone Wolf
The Outsider of the field. Plays his own game, marches to his own drum, and refuses to consult the Garmin. Brings dry humor, a long memory, and an unflappable demeanor that the rest of the field secretly envies. Finished a respectable fourth.
∞
Mystique
4
Finish
+400
Odds
+650
— Player No. IV —
Svein Anders Haugen
2025 Champion · 2026 Runner-up
Defending champion (2025). Came to Trollhättan as the man to beat and put up the strongest fight all weekend. Opened R1 with a 7 and a 10, but rallied back nine in 41 — and finished the tournament an honorable second. The closest of the chasers.
1

2

Press Row

From the Booth

Selected commentary from the broadcast booth and the assembled gentlemen of the press.

Six. Six Yellow Jackets. We are no longer asking whether gulrik is the greatest player in this tournament's history. The question is whether anyone else will ever come close. The 89 at Koberg was clinical, surgical, and — frankly — unfair to whoever shows up next year.
Bjørn-Tore HagenSkandinavisk Golf-TV · Lead Analyst
Haugen came in as defending 2025 champion and carried the entire field on his back this weekend. A 10 on the par-5 tenth at Onsjö would have broken most men. He came back, fought through Koberg with grit, and earned his runner-up trophy. That is what we call resilience.
Margareta LundqvistAftonbladet Sport · Senior Correspondent
A two-man tournament was not what anyone expected. Espen with his back, Geir with his leg — both old warriors sidelined. The Swedish Open mourns its absent gentlemen. Get well, lads. The tee box at Koberg will not be the same without you.
Patrik SundströmGolfsverige Magasin · Tour Insider

Order of Play

Tournament Schedule

Three days of competition. One Yellow Jacket. All times CET.

Thursday · April 30 · The Travel Day
14:30Departure from BekkestuaConvoy via E18 South · Customary stop at Svinesund
19:00Arrival, TrollhättanHotel check-in. Light supper. Early to bed.
Friday · May 1 · Round 1 Postponed Recap & Round 2
10:30Round 1 · Onsjö GK18 holes · Yellow Tees · Completed Thursday afternoon
12:40Round 2 · Koberg GKThe slottsbane. The decisive round. Champion crowned at the 18th.
19:00Players' DinnerChampions banquet · The Yellow Jacket displayed
Saturday · May 2 · Final Round & Departure
tbdRound 3 · Final · Course TBDThe crowning of the 2026 champion
~16:30Departure for NorwayYellow Jacket secured. Receipts retained.
